children.name: Sylvia
children.description: Scrub warblers of hedgerows, thickets, and forest edge. The Blackcap is one of Sweden's finest songsters, a rich outpouring of clear fluted notes; the Common Whitethroat perches conspicuously on bramble tops to deliver its scratchy, cheerful song.

children.name: Sylvia atricapilla
children.description: One of Sweden's finest songsters, the male's rich fluty warble pouring from woodland and gardens, the sexes told apart by black (male) or chestnut (female) caps. Increasing numbers now winter in Britain rather than the Mediterranean.

children.name: Sylvia borin
children.description: A plain, featureless brown warbler whose anonymity is offset by a sustained, mellow, babbling song from dense scrub and young woodland. A long-distance migrant wintering in sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Sylvia communis
children.description: A restless warbler of brambly hedgerows and field margins, the male delivering a scratchy song from an exposed perch or in a brief dancing song-flight. It winters in the African Sahel.

children.name: Sylvia curruca
children.description: A small, neat warbler with a dark mask and a distinctive song that ends in a dry rattle, skulking in tall hedges and thickets. It migrates south-east towards north-east Africa via the eastern Mediterranean.

children.name: Sylvia nisoria
children.description: A large, heavy warbler with a fierce yellow eye and barred underparts in the adult, often nesting near Red-backed Shrikes. It reaches the western edge of its range in south-east Sweden.

children.name: Sylvia melanocephala
children.description: A common resident warbler of Mediterranean scrub, the male with a glossy black cap, red eye-ring, and white throat; one of the most widespread breeders of southern Europe.

children.name: Sylvia undata
children.description: A small, dark, long-tailed warbler of lowland heath and Mediterranean scrub; northern populations crash in hard winters. Near Threatened in parts of its range.

children.name: Sylvia cantillans
children.description: A slim Mediterranean warbler, the male with a brick-red throat bordered by a white moustache, breeding in open woodland and scrub.

children.name: Sylvia hortensis
children.description: A large, pale-eyed warbler of open Mediterranean woodland and olive groves, with a black-capped male and rich fluty song.

children.name: Sylvia rueppelli
children.description: A scrub warbler of the eastern Mediterranean, the male with a black throat and crown separated by a white moustachial stripe.

children.name: Sylvia melanothorax
children.description: A scrub warbler endemic as a breeder to Cyprus, the male heavily spotted black below; winters in the eastern Mediterranean and Middle East.

children.name: Sylvia sarda
children.description: A grey, long-tailed Mediterranean scrub warbler breeding on Corsica, Sardinia, and nearby islands.

children.name: Sylvia conspicillata
children.description: A small, rufous-winged warbler of low coastal scrub and saltmarsh around the Mediterranean and Macaronesia.

children.name: Sylvia deserticola
children.description: A warbler of North African mountain scrub and Atlas woodland, the male warm rufous below.

children.name: Sylvia leucomelaena
children.description: A black-capped warbler of acacia scrub in the southern Levant, Arabia, and northeast Africa, often cocking and dipping its tail.

children.name: Sylvia mystacea
children.description: A scrub warbler of the Caucasus, Middle East, and Central Asia, the male with a pinkish flush and dark cap; winters in northeast Africa.

children.name: Sylvia abyssinica
children.description: A skulking warbler-like bird of African montane forest understorey, recently moved into Sylvia from the babblers.

children.name: Sylvia balearica
children.description: A grey scrub warbler endemic to the Balearic Islands, closely related to Marmora's Warbler.

children.name: Sylvia crassirostris
children.description: A large pale-eyed warbler of the eastern Mediterranean and Middle East, wintering in northeast Africa and Arabia.

children.name: Sylvia subalpina
children.description: A Mediterranean warbler split from the Subalpine Warbler, breeding on Corsica, Sardinia, and northwest Italy, identified by its distinctive rattling call.

children.name: Parisoma
children.description: Slim African warblers of acacia savanna and scrub, formerly grouped with the tit-flycatchers.

children.name: Sylvia boehmi
children.description: A small grey warbler of dry acacia bush in East Africa, marked with a narrow black breast band.

children.name: Sylvia layardi
children.description: A grey, white-eyed warbler of arid karoo scrub in southern Africa.
